name: Facility Design and Safety Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Installing high-tech safety systems increases initial capital expenditure → Systemic: 15-20% higher operational costs due to maintenance and staffing requirements → Strategic: Enhances participant safety but risks creating an artificial environment that suppresses natural parasomnia behaviors. Controls clinical rigor vs. ecological validity. Weakness: The options fail to consider long-term maintenance cost volatility in Germany's regulatory environment.
options: Prioritize modular safety upgrades with removable padding to allow flexible reconfiguration of suites for different study phases
Implement passive safety measures like motion-activated lighting and non-slip flooring to reduce technical complexity
Deploy real-time physiological pattern recognition using edge computing to prioritize high-fidelity data capture during critical events
review: Controls clinical rigor vs. ecological validity. Weakness: The options fail to consider long-term maintenance cost volatility in Germany's regulatory environment.

options: Standardize on a single sensor platform for all monitoring tiers to simplify data integration

review: Controls data completeness vs. operational throughput. Weakness: The options fail to consider interoperability challenges between legacy EEG systems and new sensor technologies.
